questions.question: Use filter_subcategories with subcategories_to_remove equal to bachelor's degree, some college, associate's degree, high school, some high school, sort_bars with order equal to descending to evaluate whether the educational level of parents shows a consistent trend across all ethnic groups of "the higher the education level → the higher the score."

answer.value: Yes. The higher the master's degree is, the higher the math score will be.

reasoning.reasoning: Sort the tools and confirm the order of the scores for each group from highest to lowest.
Using the tool to filter out the "master degree" values, it was found that the order of the group scores from highest to lowest remained unchanged.

data_filtered.filter: datum['parental level of education'] != "bachelor's degree" && datum['parental level of education'] != "some college" && datum['parental level of education'] != "associate's degree" && datum['parental level of education'] != "high school" && datum['parental level of education'] != "some high school"
